body:
## Summary
- propose a post-auth relayfile mount-session contract for sandboxes
- recommend a  golden-path API that hides token/env/process plumbing
- document the intended abstraction boundary for Daytona, E2B, and similar runtimes

## Why
Sandbox integrators should be able to attach an already-authorized workspace into a local directory with one call, without redoing login or assembling low-level relayfile mount pieces manually.

latestReviews.body:
**Actionable comments posted: 1**
🧹 Nitpick comments (2)
docs/architecture/relayfile-post-auth-mount-dx.md (2)
`116-131`: _⚡ Quick win_ **Consider exposing `expiresAt` in the handle for supervision.** The Cloud API response includes an `expiresAt` field (line 167), but the `MountedWorkspaceHandle` doesn't expose it. For sandbox supervision and diagnostics, knowing when the mount session expires would be valuable, especially given that automatic token refresh might not be supported in v1 (line 271).
🔧 Suggested type enhancement ```diff type MountedWorkspaceHandle = { workspaceId: string; localDir: string; remotePath: string; mode: "poll" | "stream"; ready: boolean; + expiresAt?: string; stop(): Promise; status(): Promise<{ ready: boolean; pid?: number; lastHeartbeatAt?: string; mode: "poll" | "stream"; + expiresAt?: string; }>; env(): Record; }; ``` This enables sandbox supervisors to proactively handle expiration rather than reacting to mount failures.
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. In `@docs/architecture/relayfile-post-auth-mount-dx.md` around lines 116 - 131, MountedWorkspaceHandle currently lacks exposure of the Cloud API's expiresAt field; update the type definition for MountedWorkspaceHandle to include an optional expiresAt?: string (or Date) property and ensure any implementations that construct or return this handle (e.g., code that builds the object returned from mount logic) populate expiresAt from the Cloud API response so supervisors can read the expiry alongside ready/status/env/stop() for proactive supervision. ```
--- `97-110`: _⚡ Quick win_ **Clarify provider verification behavior in `ensureMountedWorkspace`.** The specification states that this method "optionally verifies the requested provider is already connected" (line 108), but doesn't specify what happens when the provider is not connected. Should it throw an error, return a specific error state, or proceed anyway? Clarifying this behavior would prevent implementation inconsistencies and set clear expectations for callers.
💡 Suggested specification addition Consider adding a behavior note after line 109: ```diff - resolves or creates the requested workspace - optionally verifies the requested provider is already connected + - if provider verification fails, throws `ProviderNotConnectedError` - mounts it locally ``` Or specify alternative behaviors (skip verification, return error state, etc.) based on your design intent.
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. In `@docs/architecture/relayfile-post-auth-mount-dx.md` around lines 97 - 110, The spec for ensureMountedWorkspace currently says it "optionally verifies the requested provider is already connected" but doesn't define outcome when the provider is not connected; update the docs to explicitly state the behavior for ensureMountedWorkspace (e.g., whether it throws an error, returns a distinct error state, or mounts without verification) and document the configuration flag that controls this (reference ensureMountedWorkspace and the "provider" parameter), include a short example of each alternative outcome (error vs proceed) and the expected return shape or thrown error so implementers and callers have a clear, unambiguous contract. ```
🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. Inline comments: In `@docs/architecture/relayfile-post-auth-mount-dx.md`: - Around line 58-60: Replace the slash-separated "app/web" instances with grammatically correct wording: when used as a compound modifier (before a noun) change to a hyphenated form "app-web", otherwise use "app or web" (e.g., change "in app/web" to "in the app or web" or "app-web " as appropriate) to fix hyphenation and improve readability for the phrases shown in the diff. --- Nitpick comments: In `@docs/architecture/relayfile-post-auth-mount-dx.md`: - Around line 116-131: MountedWorkspaceHandle currently lacks exposure of the Cloud API's expiresAt field; update the type definition for MountedWorkspaceHandle to include an optional expiresAt?: string (or Date) property and ensure any implementations that construct or return this handle (e.g., code that builds the object returned from mount logic) populate expiresAt from the Cloud API response so supervisors can read the expiry alongside ready/status/env/stop() for proactive supervision. - Around line 97-110: The spec for ensureMountedWorkspace currently says it "optionally verifies the requested provider is already connected" but doesn't define outcome when the provider is not connected; update the docs to explicitly state the behavior for ensureMountedWorkspace (e.g., whether it throws an error, returns a distinct error state, or mounts without verification) and document the configuration flag that controls this (reference ensureMountedWorkspace and the "provider" parameter), include a short example of each alternative outcome (error vs proceed) and the expected return shape or thrown error so implementers and callers have a clear, unambiguous contract. ```
